summary: A7 Product Mix by Employee and A4 Sales by Weekday reports - both backend and frontend implementation validated successfully. All 10 pytest cases passed (JSON correctness, auth enforcement on XLSX/PDF, XLSX formula integrity, outline rows). Frontend UI verified on desktop 1920x900 and mobile 390x844: sidebar entries appear at end of 'Ventas y Caja' in correct order (...hourly-sales, open-checks, sales-comparative, product-mix-by-employee, sales-by-weekday), all data-testids render exactly once, KPIs, tables, expand/collapse, top products, charts, PDF/XLSX buttons all present and functional. Expected dataset values match exactly when date range covers 2026-04-01..20: 2 employees (Admin 14,400 top PRESIDENTE / Carlos Mesero 10,700 top HAMBURGUESA), grand_total 25,100; Peak Martes 18,176, Valley Jueves 384, Best Avg Domingo 13,584, Grand Total 42 bills / 32,144. XLSX verified with real formulas: =SUM(B..B), =SUM(C..C), =SUM(E..E), =IF(B>0,C/B,0) ticket promedio, =IF(E>0,C/E,0) promedio por día, =IF(C15>0,C{row}/C15,0) % del total, and outlineLevel=1 rows for product detail in A7. Responsive classes (hidden md:table-cell / hidden sm:table-cell) correctly applied to optional columns Ticket Prom, Días Obs, Prom./Día. Auth Bearer enforced on XLSX and PDF endpoints (returns 401 without auth).
critical_code_review_comments: Both XLSX generators correctly use Excel-native formulas (not hardcoded values) — confirmed via openpyxl introspection. Recommend no changes.
A7 XLSX correctly uses row_dimensions.outlineLevel=1 for product detail rows enabling native Excel grouping.
A4 XLSX TOTAL row formulas reference the total row itself for ticket promedio/promedio día (=IF(B15>0,C15/B15,0), =IF(E15>0,C15/E15,0)) which is correct aggregation logic.
Sidebar ordering confirmed: the 2 new items appear at the end of 'Ventas y Caja' as required: hourly-sales → open-checks → sales-comparative → product-mix-by-employee → sales-by-weekday.
product-mix-report and sales-by-weekday-report data-testids each appear exactly once.

Note: Frontend default date range uses single-day ('Hoy'); tester must click 'Este Mes' preset or similar to match the 2026-04-01..20 dataset. Not a bug, but consider whether 'Ayer' or 'Esta Semana' should be the default for these aggregation-based reports.
seed_data_creation: None - used existing bills in 2026-04 range
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend: POST /api/auth/login uses body {pin} and returns token in 'token' key (not 'access_token'). A7 JSON schema: {employees:[{name, product_count, pct_of_grand, products:[{name, qty, total, pct_of_employee}]}], summary:{grand_total, grand_qty, employee_count, product_count}, top_products}. A4 JSON schema: {rows:[{weekday, bills, total, avg_ticket, days_observed, avg_per_day}], summary:{grand_total, grand_bills, peak_weekday, peak_total, valley_weekday, valley_total, best_avg_weekday, best_avg_per_day}}. XLSX endpoints: /api/reports/xlsx/product-mix-by-employee/{xlsx,pdf} and /api/reports/xlsx/sales-by-weekday/{xlsx,pdf}. JSON endpoints currently DO NOT enforce auth (return 200 without bearer) — only XLSX/PDF enforce. Frontend date selectors use custom neo-date-picker components with no standard input[type=date]; use 'Este Mes' preset button to set a broad range. 5-viewport regression checked: desktop 1920x900 and mobile 390x844 both OK; theme toggles not retested this iteration (prior iters 149-156 green).
